title: Black Hole as Spacetime Geometry
definition: A black hole is not a material object in spacetime but a self-sustaining, non-linear geometric structure of spacetime itself. Mass and spin define the object, reversing the ordinary relationship where spin is a property of an object.

proofs.description: Extracted from conversation via CADMIES Harvest Pipeline
proofs.reference: CADMIES Harvest Pipeline v4.0 — raw dump rescue

difficulty_levels.beginner: A black hole isn't a thing spinning in space — it IS spinning space.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A black hole is a self-sustaining, non-linear geometric structure of spacetime. Mass and spin define the object rather than being properties of it.
difficulty_levels.expert: In the Kerr solution, the black hole is a vacuum spacetime geometry where mass and angular momentum are global properties of the metric itself. The usual object-property relationship is inverted: the geometry is primary, and what we call mass and spin are parameters of that geometry.

extra_fields.insight: The role of cause and effect are reversed in a black hole compared to ordinary objects. The geometry comes first.
extra_fields.historical_context: The discovery of black holes as geometries in spacetime is a significant development in our understanding of general relativity and its implications for the universe.
limitations: Black hole geometries are difficult to observe directly due to their extreme densities and the effects of event horizons
applications: Helping us understand the behavior of matter under extreme gravity
Aiding in our understanding of the structure and evolution of galaxies
Providing a testbed for theories of quantum gravity
